title: Bump common to v0.66.1
body:
Bump common to v0.66.1 in preparation for Podman v5.7

<!--- Please read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/containers/container-libs/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) before proceeding --->

comments.body: Is there going to be a release created with this?

I think we want a github actions for the release that attaches the tarball as request in https://github.com/containers/container-libs/issues/485

I guess we could do it manually now but go only needs the tag so there is no strict requirement for a "release" I think?

Historically the c/* libs haven’t attracted much effort on writing _good_ release notes… so a GitHub release page with mostly-auto-generated notes is IMHO not all that valuable.

I agree #485 would be good to have.
My main concern with an official release was the `containers-common` rpm. If there are no (significant) changes in the config files, then release is not a priority for me.
There are a few minor documentation fixes in the release, so if you need that to trigger a packit update sure let's do that

> There are a few minor documentation fixes in the release, so if you need that to trigger a packit update sure let's do that

If it's just minor doc fixes, I don't mind keeping this low priority. I'll defer to you all.
